concepts: failed-breakdown continuation after fast value reclaim
compression release with retained higher-value acceptance

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Recent visible history is still overwhelmingly explore-heavy, and the latest failure cluster spans BTC/4h, DOGE/4h, BANANA/1h, SOL/1d, and the failed ETH/4h exploit turn. That means another exploit turn is not earned, and repeating those same lanes would be lazy. Stay inside the supported universe and rotate to TAO 4h with three materially different but density-aware structures: one failed-breakdown continuation branch that only turns on after downside excess is reclaimed back above fast value with directional strength already improving, one compression-release branch that requires retained higher-value acceptance instead of ceremonial breakout stacking, and one shallow-pullback trend-reset branch that enters once a medium anchor is recovered under still-supportive crowding context. Do not clone ETH tail-harvester or ETH channel logic, do not recycle the dead BTC/DOGE rejection grammar, and do not write ultra-rare 4h event stacks that cannot realistically clear the 15-trade floor.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 15, and max_drawdown_pct <= 10.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.00, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: recent history remains explore-heavy, the last visible failure cluster spans multiple lanes and does not justify another exploit turn, and TAO/4h is a clean supported rotation away from the recent ETH/BTC/DOGE/BANANA/SOL paths without reusing a currently passing family.
